signature_wines.name: Sassicaia
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 8 to 12 years, cellar 25 to 50
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Cassis and graphite layered over violet aromatics. The 1985 vintage was the first Italian wine to receive a perfect 100 points from Robert Parker (Wine Advocate, 1992). Source: tenutasanguido.com and robertparker.com.
signature_wines.description: The founding Super Tuscan. Bordeaux cuttings planted at Bolgheri in 1944 by Mario Incisa della Rocchetta; granted its own single-estate DOC in 1994.

signature_wines.name: Tignanello
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 5 to 10 years, cellar 20 to 30
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Violet and dark cherry above graphite, with firm tannins and a long savoury finish. Source: antinori.it and Wine Advocate 97 points 2019 vintage.
signature_wines.description: The Super Tuscan that broke Chianti Classico DOCG rules in 1971 by blending Sangiovese with Cabernet and ageing in French barrique. Still a global benchmark.

signature_wines.name: Solaia
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 8 to 12 years, cellar 25 to 35
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Cassis and graphite intensity over a long Tuscan acid spine. Wine Spectator named the 1997 vintage its Wine of the Year for 2000 (scored 98 points). Source: antinori.it and Wine Spectator Top 100 archive.
signature_wines.description: Cabernet-led counterpart to Tignanello, from the same Tenuta Tignanello hillside. The 1997 vintage was the first Italian wine ever named Wine Spectator's Wine of the Year (2000, 98 points).

signature_wines.name: Ornellaia
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 8 to 12 years, cellar 20 to 35
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Black cherry, cassis and sweet cedar built on a graphite-iron mineral spine. Wine Spectator named the 1998 vintage its Wine of the Year for 2001 (scored 96 points). Source: ornellaia.com and Wine Spectator Top 100 archive.
signature_wines.description: Lodovico Antinori's Bolgheri Superiore DOC Bordeaux-blend flagship, founded 1981 and now owned by Frescobaldi. A coastal-Tuscan benchmark for the Bordeaux idiom.

signature_wines.name: Masseto
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 10 to 15 years, cellar 25 to 35
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Black cherry, graphite, sweet cedar over polished oak and salinity. Source: masseto.com and Wine Advocate 100 points for the 2015 vintage (review 2018).
signature_wines.description: Italy's most prestigious Merlot. A 7-hectare blue-clay parcel inside the Ornellaia estate, with its own dedicated cellar since 2020. A reference Merlot worldwide.

signature_wines.name: Biondi-Santi Brunello di Montalcino Riserva
signature_wines.style: still red Brunello di Montalcino Riserva
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 15 to 20 years, cellar 30 to 60
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Dried cherry, rose petal, iron and leather across an extraordinarily long finish. Famous library vintages 1955, 1964, 1975, 1988, 2010, 2016. Source: biondisanti.it.
signature_wines.description: The founding Brunello Riserva, released only in exceptional vintages from the 1888-bottled estate. EPI Group ownership since 2017. Famous library vintages 1955.

signature_wines.name: Soldera Case Basse Toscana IGT
signature_wines.style: still red pure Sangiovese cult IGT
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 12 to 15 years, cellar 25 to 40
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Dried cherry, potpourri, rose petal and iron across a savoury herbal core. Source: soldera.it. Pre-2013 the estate bottled under Brunello DOCG; the 2007 to 2012 Brunello vintages were destroyed in the December 2012 cellar vandalism.
signature_wines.description: Cult Montalcino estate that left Brunello DOCG in 2013. Bottles continue as Toscana IGT from the original Case Basse and Intistieti vineyards.

signature_wines.name: Casanova di Neri Cerretalto
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 12 to 15 years, cellar 20 to 35
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Black cherry, graphite, leather and violet with espresso and polished oak. Source: consorziobrunellodimontalcino.it and Wine Advocate 98 points 2016 vintage (Monica Larner review).
signature_wines.description: Single-vineyard Brunello from a south-facing parcel above the Asso river. Casanova di Neri's most concentrated cru. One of the iconic Tuscan bottles in 2026.

signature_wines.name: Argiano Brunello di Montalcino
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 8 to 12 years, cellar 20 to 30
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Black cherry, graphite, leather and violet across a sweet-spice mid-palate. Wine Spectator named the 2018 vintage its 2023 Wine of the Year (scored 95 points). Source: argiano.net and Wine Spectator Top 100 archive.
signature_wines.description: Argiano's annata Brunello. The 2018 vintage was named Wine Spectator's 2023 Wine of the Year (95 points), the first Italian wine to claim the title in two decades.

signature_wines.name: Fontodi Flaccianello della Pieve
signature_wines.style: still red pure Sangiovese Super Tuscan
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 5 to 10 years, cellar 20 to 30
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Black cherry, violet, Mediterranean herbs and graphite with dark plum and balsamic depth. Source: fontodi.com and Wine Advocate 98 points 2019 vintage (Monica Larner, February 2023).
signature_wines.description: Giovanni Manetti's flagship pure Sangiovese Toscana IGT from the Conca d'Oro at Panzano. Helped legitimise 100 percent Sangiovese as a Super Tuscan idiom.

signature_wines.name: Tua Rita Redigaffi
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 10 to 12 years, cellar 20 to 30
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Black cherry, graphite, violet and sweet cedar over polished oak. Source: tuarita.it and Wine Advocate 100 points 2000 vintage.
signature_wines.description: Tua Rita's cult 100 percent Merlot from Suvereto. The 2000 vintage was awarded 100 points by Wine Advocate, putting the Val di Cornia on the world map.

signature_wines.name: Avignonesi Occhio di Pernice Vin Santo
signature_wines.style: dessert passito Vin Santo from Sangiovese
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Dried fig, tamarind, walnut, candied orange and burnt caramel with rancio depth. Raisined Sangiovese aged 10 plus years in caratelli. Source: avignonesi.it.
signature_wines.description: The cult Vin Santo. Raisined Sangiovese aged 10 plus years in caratelli; widely considered Italy's most ambitious sweet wine. The Saverys Demeter-biodynamic.

signature_wines.name: Le Pergole Torte
signature_wines.style: still red pure Sangiovese Toscana IGT
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 8 to 10 years, cellar 20 to 30
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Red cherry, rose petal and dried herbs over a tobacco-and-iron core; very long, linear finish. Source: montevertine.it. The first pure Sangiovese vinified in the Chianti zone, released 1977.
signature_wines.description: Sergio Manetti's pioneering pure-Sangiovese Toscana IGT, first released 1977 from Radda in Chianti. The canonical IGT-by-choice Sangiovese.

signature_wines.name: Petrolo Galatrona
signature_wines.style: still red 100 percent Val d'Arno di Sopra DOC Merlot
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 8 to 12 years, cellar 18 to 25
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Black cherry, blackberry and violet over a graphite-iron mineral spine. Source: petrolo.it. Italy's reference 100 percent Merlot away from Bolgheri; first released 1994.
signature_wines.description: Petrolo's Val d'Arno di Sopra DOC Galatrona: 100 percent Merlot from the 10-hectare blue-clay parcel at Mercatale Valdarno. First vintage 1994.

signature_wines.name: Poggio di Sotto Brunello di Montalcino Riserva
signature_wines.style: still red Brunello di Montalcino DOCG Riserva
signature_wines.vintage_range: approachable from 12 to 15 years, cellar 25 to 40
signature_wines.tasting_notes: Dried cherry, rose petal, leather and dried fig with iron and balsamic depth; very long finish. Source: poggiodisotto.it and Wine Advocate 98 points 2015 vintage. Released only in exceptional vintages.
signature_wines.description: Poggio di Sotto's Brunello Riserva from Castelnuovo dell'Abate, around 60 months in large Slavonian botti. Released only in exceptional vintages; ColleMassari Group-owned since 2011.
